task_completion.description: Verify that new partition walls have been drawn inside the existing building shell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan pane, there should be new interior walls dividing the open space into multiple distinct sections.

task_completion.description: Verify that room areas have been explicitly defined within the partitioned spaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan pane, the floor areas of the newly created spaces should be shaded (typically light blue or grey) indicating that 'Create rooms' tool was used.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required zones are clearly labeled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels must be visible in the 2D plan for the required areas: control room, equipment staging and storage area, training room, crew break room, and staff restrooms.

task_completion.description: Verify that the zones are furnished for professional use.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Furniture items (e.g., desks, chairs, shelves, bathroom fixtures) should be visible in both the 2D plan and the 3D view pane, placed logically within the newly created rooms.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project was saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar should display 'solar_center.sh3d', or a file explorer/terminal window should show 'solar_center.sh3d' located in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to complete the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active use of its tools (wall drawing, catalog dragging), rather than terminal scripts generating XML files.

integrity.description: Ensure the 3D view accurately reflects the 2D plan modifications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom 3D view pane in Sweet Home 3D should show the 3D models of the walls and furniture placed in the 2D plan, proving genuine application rendering.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent built upon the starter file rather than downloading a completed solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The layout of the outer building shell should match the original starter file's residential villa shell, just with new interior walls and furniture added.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying an empty building shell ('solar_facility_starter.sh3d') into a functional facility. The verifier strictly checks the exported XML for 4 features: new wall creation (20 pts), room definition (20 pts), label placement for the 5 specific zones (25 pts), and furniture placement (20 pts). The final file must be saved as 'solar_center.sh3d' (15 pts). Look for visual confirmation of these elements in the Sweet Home 3D interface.
